references.text: [Er82c] Erd\H{o}s, P., Miscellaneous problems in number theory. Congr. Numer. (1982), 25-45.

remarks.text: A problem of Erd\H{o}s and Selfridge, who proved (see \cite{Er82c})\[h(n) \asymp \left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right)^{1/2}.\]A heuristic of Tao using the Cram\'{e}r model for the primes (detailed in the comments) suggests this is true with\[c=\sqrt{2\pi}=2.506\cdots.\]

statement.text:
If\[n! = \prod_i p_i^{k_i}\]is the factorisation into distinct primes then let $h(n)$ count the number of distinct exponents $k_i$. 

Prove that there exists some $c>0$ such that\[h(n) \sim c \left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right)^{1/2}\]as $n\to \infty$.
